prerequisites: A Babelforce account with access to generate API credentials.
A Babelforce API access key ID and access token (generated in Babelforce).
The Babelforce region/environment endpoint to connect to, as listed in the Regions and environments section of the Babelforce API documentation.
Optional: a start and/or end date (epoch Unix timestamp) to bound the imported data.

authMethods.name: API access key ID and token
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Babelforce access key ID (access_key_id) and access token (access_token). Both are required secret credentials generated in Babelforce and supplied to the connector together.

setupSteps.title: Generate Babelforce API credentials
setupSteps.body: In Babelforce, generate an API access key ID and access token following the Babelforce API documentation. These two values are the credentials the connector uses to authenticate.

setupSteps.title: Select your region
setupSteps.body: Set the required region field to your Babelforce environment endpoint, using one of the values listed in the Regions and environments section of the Babelforce API documentation.

setupSteps.title: Provide the credentials
setupSteps.body: Enter the access_key_id and access_token into the connector. Both are required and are stored as secrets.

setupSteps.title: Optionally bound the date range
setupSteps.body: If you want to limit imported records, set date_created_from and/or date_created_to as epoch Unix timestamps. These fields are optional; leave them empty to import without a date bound.
